km.Burghwallis, Yorkshire, England: ប៊ើហ្គhវ៉លីស, យ៉ុរកស៊ីរe, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois於大約1115年出生。她於1135年之前與John de Burgo在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois于大约1115年出生。她于1135年之前与John de Burgo在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Blois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille.
French and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Blois in Loire-et-Cher and, in some cases possibly also from a place of the same name in Jura. Compare   Deblois  .
French: nickname from the Old French adjective blois ‘lisping’.

variants.name: Bloise
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rolando, Salvatore, Biagio, Carmelo, Giulio.  Spanish Alejandro, Ines, Jose, Juan Antonio, Luis.
Italian (Calabria): from French Blois . The surname Bloise is also found in southern France (Bouches-du-Rhône) and in the Dominican Republic.
English (Suffolk): variant of   Blois  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Deblois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Rosaire, Amelie, Damien, Emile, Fernand, Herve, Jacques, Jean Guy, Monique, Ovide.
French: habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone from Blois (see Blois ).

nameMeanings.name: Madeleine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The French form of the byname of a character in the New Testament, Mary Magdalene ‘Mary of Magdala’. Magdala was a village on Lake Galilee, a few miles north of Tiberias. The woman ‘which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities’ (Luke 8:2) was given this name in the Bible to distinguish her from other bearers of the very common name Mary . It was widely accepted in Christian folk belief that she was the same person as the repentant sinner who washed Christ's feet with her tears in the previous chapter (Luke 7), but there is no support in the text for this identification.

variants.name: Madaline
meanings: The French form of the byname of a character in the New Testament, Mary Magdalene ‘Mary of Magdala’. Magdala was a village on Lake Galilee, a few miles north of Tiberias. The woman ‘which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities’ (Luke 8:2) was given this name in the Bible to distinguish her from other bearers of the very common name Mary . It was widely accepted in Christian folk belief that she was the same person as the repentant sinner who washed Christ's feet with her tears in the previous chapter (Luke 7), but there is no support in the text for this identification.

variants.name: Madeline
meanings: A female name softened down from Magdalen. Compare the French Madeleine and Madelon; the Italian Maddalena; and the Spanish Madelena.

variants.name: Madalene
meanings: The French form of the byname of a character in the New Testament, Mary Magdalene ‘Mary of Magdala’. Magdala was a village on Lake Galilee, a few miles north of Tiberias. The woman ‘which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities’ (Luke 8:2) was given this name in the Bible to distinguish her from other bearers of the very common name Mary . It was widely accepted in Christian folk belief that she was the same person as the repentant sinner who washed Christ's feet with her tears in the previous chapter (Luke 7), but there is no support in the text for this identification.

meanings: The French form of the byname of a character in the New Testament, Mary Magdalene ‘Mary of Magdala’. Magdala was a village on Lake Galilee, a few miles north of Tiberias. The woman ‘which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities’ (Luke 8:2) was given this name in the Bible to distinguish her from other bearers of the very common name Mary . It was widely accepted in Christian folk belief that she was the same person as the repentant sinner who washed Christ's feet with her tears in the previous chapter (Luke 7), but there is no support in the text for this identification.

variants.name: Madelaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Madeleine .

variants.name: Madoline
meanings: Modern respelling of   Madeleine .

variants.name: Madlyn
meanings: Modern variant of Madeleine , influenced by the productive suffix -lyn; see Lynn .

variants.name: Madelyn
meanings: The French form of the byname of a character in the New Testament, Mary Magdalene ‘Mary of Magdala’. Magdala was a village on Lake Galilee, a few miles north of Tiberias. The woman ‘which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities’ (Luke 8:2) was given this name in the Bible to distinguish her from other bearers of the very common name Mary . It was widely accepted in Christian folk belief that she was the same person as the repentant sinner who washed Christ's feet with her tears in the previous chapter (Luke 7), but there is no support in the text for this identification.

en.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois was born about 1115. She married John de Burgo before 1135, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois est née environ 1115. Elle a épousé John de Burgo avant 1135, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois wurde um 1115 geboren. Sie hat vor 1135 in Europa John de Burgo geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois è nata il circa 1115. Lei sposato John de Burgo prima del 1135, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Burghwallis, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアブルグhワリス
ja.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Bloisは約1115年に生まれた。1135年以前にヨーロッパでJohn de Burgoと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois(은)는 대략 1115년 태어났다. 그녀는 1135년 이전 우럽에서 John de Burgo(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois nasceu aproximadamente 1115. Ela casou-se com John de Burgo antes 1135, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois родилась около 1115 г. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Burgo перед 1135 г. в местности Европа. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Madeleine de Blois nació aproximadamente 1115. Se casó con John de Burgo antes de 1135, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.

th.Burghwallis, Yorkshire, England: เบอรกฮวัลลิส, โยอรกชิเร, เอิงแลนด์
